title: Water Resources Management (Water Well Quality Monitoring) Regulations, 2018
title_en: Water Resources Management (Water Well Quality Monitoring) Regulations, 2018

provisions.title: Citation
provisions.content: These Regulations may be cited as the Water Resources Management (Water Well Quality Monitoring) Regulations, 2018.

provisions.title: Application
provisions.content: These Regulations shall apply in relation to water well quality monitoring.

provisions.title: Interpretation
provisions.content: In these Regulations, unless the context otherwise requires: “ Act ” means the Water Resources Management Act; [Cap. 331] “ Board ” shall have the meaning as ascribed to it under the Act ; “ certificate of water quality test ” means a legal verification for the compliance with Part II of these Regulations. “ water well ” means a hole which is dug, constructed or drilled for the purpose of abstracting groundwater; “ water well owner ” means a person who owns the well or a person who is in the actual occupation of a premises in which a well exist; “ toxic chemicals ” means any substance including heavy metals, pesticides, Polychlorinated Biphenyls and dioxin which can harm human being and environment by causing carcinogenic, mutagenic or death Part II – Water well quality monitoring

provisions.title: Shallow water well
provisions.content: (1) A shallow water well constructed in the township areas may be considered not fit for human consumptions where circumstances permit. (2) A shallow water well in village area shall not be deemed unfit for human consumption where it has not been so proved by the Board or local government authorities as the case may be. (3) Nothing under sub-regulation (1) , shall be construed as to waive an obligation to the well owner to ensure quality of such water. (4) For the purpose of this regulation, a shallow water well means a well dug with a depth not exceeding fifteen meters.

provisions.title: Obligation to test water well quality
provisions.content: (1) A water well owner shall not use or permit to be used well water for human consumption unless the quality of such water has been tested by the relevant authority. (2) The Schedule to these Regulations specify water quality key parameters and frequency of testing. (3) A water well owner shall be required to conduct a water quality test in accordance with the standards issued by the Tanzania Bureau Standards under the Standards Act . [Cap. 130] (4) The cost for sampling and water quality test shall be borne by water well owner . (5) The cost for monitoring of water well quality shall be borne by the Board or local government authority as the case may be.

provisions.title: Water quality certification
provisions.content: (1) There shall be issued a certificate of water quality test by a relevant authority upon being satisfied that the quality of such water is fit for human consumption. (2) A water well owner shall display a certificate of water quality test in a conspicuous place at his premises for inspection. (3) For the purpose of this regulation, a term “fit for human consumption” means quality of water which complies with latest version of drinking water standard issued by the Tanzania Bureau Standards under the Standards Act . [Cap. 130]

provisions.title: Inspection of wells
provisions.content: The Board shall conduct an inspection of wells within its area of jurisdiction for the purposes of ascertaining quality of well water and validity of water quality certificate for the period of not more than six months or as may be determined by the Board .

provisions.title: Offence
provisions.content: A well owner who contravenes the provisions of this part commits an offence. Part III – Obligation of the parties

provisions.title: Obligation to register well
provisions.content: (1) A well owner shall not use a well unless it is registered by the Board . (2) Possession of the water use permit issued under the Act shall be evidence of the registration of the well under these Regulations.

provisions.title: Guidelines
provisions.content: The Minister may, for better carrying out of provisions of these Regulations, issue guidelines prescribing technical requirements of water well or any other matter as may be determined from time to time. Part IV – Well for water supply

provisions.title: Condition to supply well water to public
provisions.content: (1). Where a person intends to construct a well for the purpose of supplying water to the public, the owner shall seek for approval the relevant authority. (2) Where a person intends to change use of water use permit to supply water to public, he shall seek approval of relevant water authority. (3) Nothing under this Part shall bar a water authority or Local Government Authority of its right to redeem an obligation to supply water to public where it has satisfied itself of its capacity to do so, by notifying the Board for cessation of permit issued to well owner. Part V – General provision

provisions.title: Unfitness of well
provisions.content: A water well shall be: (a) sealed and abandoned where a water quality test result indicates unfitness due to the contents of toxic chemicals ; or (b) permitted to be used where water quality test result indicates unfitness due to other factors than toxic chemicals , subject to proper treatment.

provisions.title: General penalty
provisions.content: A person who contravenes the provisions of these Regulations commits an offence and shall, upon conviction, be liable to a fine of not less than five hundred thousands shillings or imprisonment for a term not exceeding six months or both.

provisions.title: Transitional provision
provisions.content: An existing well owner shall, within six months after coming into force of these Regulations, comply with the provisions of these Regulations. Schedule (Made under regulation 5(2)) Water quality test key parameters (1) Physical examination Parameter Unit (a) Colour TCU (b) Turbidity NTU (c) Total Dissolved Solids (TDS) mg/l (d) pH (e) Conductivity micro simen (f) Temperature Degree Centigrade (2) Chemical examination S/N Parameter Unit (a) Salinity mg/l (b) Hardness mg/l (c) Iron mg/l (d) Nitrate mg/l (e) Manganese mg/l (f) Sulphate mg/l (g) Chloride mg/l (g) Heavy metals mg/l 3. Microbiology examination Parameter Unit (a) Total Coliform Coliform count per 100 at 37° (b) Fecal Coliform Coliform count per 100 ml at 44° (c) Escherichia Coli Ecoli count per 100 ml at 44° History of this document 20 April 2018 this version Published in Gazette of the United Republic of Tanzania Commenced 14 March 2018 Assented to Related documents More documents like this one [] [] [] [] ▲ To the top > Contact us Visit our Twitter/X profile Visit our Linked In profile Visit our YouTube channel About TanzLII TanzLII is operated by the Judiciary of Tanzania and the Office of the Attorney General, Chief Parliamentary Draftsman, and publishes the law of Tanzania for free online access for all.
